Assembly: The assembly ilEpiTede1.hap1.1 is based on 64x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 25 (5.4 - 5.6 Mbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 24 (4.1 - 4.6 Mbp) are unknown. A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 1 (13.0-18.4 Mbp), chromosome 11 (8.1-11.2 Mbp), chromosome 15 (3.0-7.0 Mbp). A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 18 (4.4-7.5 Mbp). Multiple rearrangements (inversions, translocations) was observed on chromosome 6. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).

Assembly: The assembly icOcyOlen1.2 is based on 40x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Order and orientation of scaffolds in the centromeric regions is less certain than in the rest of the assembly.

Assembly: The assembly ilPhyUlic4.1 is based on 72x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based on BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements ( https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).PacBio and Hi-C data were generated from different, unsexed individuals. In the haploid assembly, the Z chromosome appears at approximately half the PacBio read coverage. In addition, numerous unlocalised scaffolds show half coverage and lack Hi-C signal. These scaffolds likely represent fragmented W chromosome sequences, consistent with the PacBio data originating from a male and the Hi-C data from a female.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Hymenoptera; Apocrita; Aculeata; Apoidea; Anthophila; Megachilidae; Megachilinae; Megachile
Assembly: 'The assembly was produced with hifiasm in Hi-C mode, decontaminated with FCS-GX, haplotigs removed with purge_dups, and scaffolded with YaHS. Pretext maps for curation were obtained with sanger-tol/curationpretext and visualised/edited with sanger-tol/pretextview and sanger-tol/agp-tpf-utils. The resulting scaffolds are mostly gapless, with 16 scaffolds matching the known karyotype for this genus (n=16), and containing all of the identified BUSCOs (>99%, hymenoptera_odb10). These likely represent the euchromatic arms of each chromosome, and accordingly most contain telomeric sequences on one end only. The assembly also contains a large number of scaffolds that could not be assigned to chromosomes, likely representing the heterochromatic and highly repetitive fraction of this genome. Mitochondria was assembled with Oatk. The assembly coverage is 33x for PacBio HiFi and 67x for Illumina Hi-C.'.

Assembly: The assembly ilBucDema1.hap1.1 is based on 76x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements~(https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).

Assembly: The assembly of Myrichthys ocellatus (fMyrOce1) is based on 55X PacBio data and 176X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 1 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 0.045 Mb (with the largest being 0.045 Mb). Additionally, 193 regions totaling 28.781 Mb (with the largest being 3.542 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly lpSchTabe1.hap1.1 is based on 58x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. Assembly: The assembly cbPohNuta1.1 is based on 104x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This assembly appears to suggest that this species is a degraded tetraploid with weak interacting HiC signal between putative homeologous chromosomes. Multiple inversions can be observed between the homeologs, and also large fusion/fission events. Chromosome 15 was split into two homeologs, chromosome 22 and 8. In addition chromosome 8 was fused to a new chromosome and there is also chromosome 21, which seems to not have a homeolog.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z assigned based on synteny to GCA_964035005.1 (Erebia palarica).
